description: Integrate with popular CI/CD platforms (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins) to enable automated deployment of changes made by DevFlow.
workflow_rationale: This is a multi-service feature that requires integrating with external CI/CD platforms. It follows the FEATURE workflow: build core integration infrastructure first, then platform-specific implementations, then monitoring, and finally integration with the orchestrator.

phases.name: Base Integration Infrastructure
phases.description: Create the base CI/CD integration infrastructure with abstract interfaces and common functionality

subtasks.description: Create base PipelineIntegration abstract class with common interface

verification.command: python -c "from devflow.integrations.base import PipelineIntegration; print('Base class OK')"

subtasks.description: Add CI/CD configuration to settings.py
Create PipelineStatus dataclass and state tracking in StateTracker

verification.command: python -c "from devflow.core.state_tracker import PipelineStatus; print('PipelineStatus OK')"

subtasks.notes:
Successfully implemented PipelineStatus enum and state tracking in StateTracker. Added:
- PipelineStatus enum with 7 states (PENDING, QUEUED, RUNNING, COMPLETED, FAILED, CANCELLED, SKIPPED)
- create_pipeline() method to register pipeline executions
- update_pipeline_status() method to update pipeline state
- get_pipeline_status() and get_all_pipelines() methods
- Updated get_metrics() to include pipeline statistics
- Updated save/load/reset methods to handle pipeline state

All verification tests passed successfully.

phases.name: GitHub Actions Integration
phases.description: Implement GitHub Actions integration with webhook support and API calls

subtasks.description: Create GitHubActions integration class

verification.command: python -c "from devflow.integrations.github_actions import GitHubActions; print('GitHubActions OK')"

subtasks.notes:
Successfully implemented GitHubActions integration class with:
- trigger_pipeline() to trigger GitHub Actions workflows via workflow_dispatch API
- get_pipeline_status() to monitor workflow run status
- get_pipeline_logs() to fetch workflow logs
- cancel_pipeline() to cancel running workflows
- Automatic workflow ID discovery
- Retry logic with configurable attempts and delays
- Comprehensive error handling for authentication and rate limiting
- Health check endpoint to verify API connectivity
- Proper status mapping from GitHub Actions to PipelineStatus enum
- Thread-safe run tracking with locks
- Support for custom workflow names and IDs
- Proper logging at all levels

All verification tests passed successfully. Updated __init__.py to export GitHubActions class.

subtasks.description: Implement GitHub Actions workflow trigger via API

verification.instructions: Review code for trigger_workflow() method with proper API call to GitHub

subtasks.notes:
Successfully implemented trigger_workflow() method in GitHubActions class:
- Accepts workflow_id (ID or filename), branch, inputs, and commit_sha parameters
- Automatically resolves workflow filenames to IDs using _get_workflow_id()
- Makes POST request to GitHub Actions workflow_dispatch API endpoint: /repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/workflows/{workflow_id}/dispatches
- Properly constructs payload with 'ref' (branch) and 'inputs' dictionary
- Waits 2 seconds for GitHub to register the workflow run
- Retrieves triggered workflow run details via GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/runs
- Creates and returns PipelineRun object with complete metadata (run_id, status, url, etc.)
- Registers the run using _register_run() for tracking
- Comprehensive error handling with try/except blocks for ValueError and ConnectionError
- Detailed docstring with parameter descriptions, return type, raises, and usage examples
- Refactored trigger_pipeline() to delegate to trigger_workflow() for consistency
- Follows existing code patterns and conventions
- No debug statements (verified: no print() or console.log)

All verification checks passed:
✓ Method exists and is callable
✓ Has proper exception handling
✓ Docstring mentions API
✓ Makes proper API calls to GitHub Actions endpoints
✓ Returns PipelineRun object with all required fields

subtasks.description: Implement GitHub Actions status monitoring

verification.instructions: Review code for get_workflow_status() method with proper status polling

subtasks.notes:
Successfully implemented get_workflow_status() method with comprehensive status monitoring:
- Returns detailed workflow status information including timestamps, actor, jobs, and metadata
- Optional polling capability with configurable timeout (default 600s) and interval (default 10s)
- Proper error handling for network errors during polling with automatic retries
- Thread-safe updates to active_runs registry using locks
- Fetches job-level status information when available
- Comprehensive documentation with usage examples
- Status change logging during polling for visibility
- Timeout handling with clear error messages
- Delegates get_pipeline_status() to get_workflow_status() for consistency
- Handles both completed (uses conclusion) and in-progress workflows
- Follows existing code patterns and conventions
- No debug statements (verified: no print() or console.log)

All verification checks passed:
✓ Method exists and is callable
✓ Has proper exception handling (ValueError, ConnectionError, TimeoutError)
✓ Comprehensive docstring with parameter descriptions and examples
✓ Makes proper API calls to GitHub Actions endpoints
✓ Returns detailed status dictionary with all required fields
✓ Supports polling with timeout and interval parameters
✓ Thread-safe updates to active_runs registry

phases.name: GitLab CI Integration

subtasks.notes:
Successfully implemented GitLabCI integration class with:

subtasks.description: Implement GitLab pipeline trigger via API

verification.instructions: Review code for trigger_pipeline() method with proper API call to GitLab

subtasks.notes:
Successfully verified trigger_pipeline() method in GitLabCI class:
- Accepts branch, parameters (key-value dict), and commit_sha parameters
- Makes POST request to GitLab API v4 endpoint: /projects/{encoded_project_path}/pipeline
- Properly constructs payload with 'ref' (branch) and optional 'sha' (commit SHA)
- Converts parameters dict to GitLab variables format with 'key' and 'value' fields
- Extracts pipeline information from API response (id, status, web_url, sha, ref, etc.)
- Creates and returns PipelineRun object with complete metadata including:
  - run_id, platform, repository, branch
  - Status mapped from GitLab to PipelineStatus enum
  - Timestamps (created_at, updated_at)
  - Web URL for pipeline in GitLab UI
  - Metadata with SHA, ref, source, variables, and triggered_by
- Registers the run using _register_run() for tracking
- Comprehensive error handling with try/except blocks for ValueError and ConnectionError
- Detailed docstring with parameter descriptions, return type, raises, and usage examples
- Proper logging at info and error levels
- Thread-safe implementation using locks
- Retry logic via _make_request() for network failures
- Follows existing code patterns and conventions
- No debug statements (verified: no print() or console.log)

All verification checks passed:
✓ Method exists and is callable
✓ Has proper exception handling
✓ Docstring describes API integration
✓ Makes proper POST request to GitLab API endpoint
✓ Returns PipelineRun object with all required fields
✓ Supports pipeline variables and custom commit SHA

phases.name: Jenkins Integration
phases.description: Implement Jenkins integration with job triggering and build monitoring

subtasks.description: Create Jenkins integration class

verification.command: python -c "from devflow.integrations.jenkins import Jenkins; print('Jenkins OK')"

subtasks.notes:
Successfully implemented Jenkins integration class with comprehensive functionality:

Core Methods:
- __init__(): Initialize Jenkins integration with API token authentication
- _create_session(): Configure requests session with Basic auth (username:token or Bearer token)
- _make_request(): Make authenticated API requests with retry logic
- trigger_job(): Trigger specific Jenkins jobs with parameters via buildWithParameters API
- trigger_pipeline(): Generic interface that delegates to trigger_job()
- get_build_status(): Get detailed build information including status, result, duration, timestamps
- get_pipeline_status(): Generic interface that returns PipelineStatus enum
- get_pipeline_logs(): Fetch console output from builds (consoleText endpoint)
- cancel_pipeline(): Cancel running builds or queued items
- _map_status(): Map Jenkins statuses to PipelineStatus enum
- health_check(): Verify Jenkins API connectivity and version

Authentication:
- Supports username:token format (Basic auth)
- Supports API token directly (Bearer auth)
- Configurable via PipelineConfig.api_token and additional_params['username']

Jenkins API Integration:
- POST /job/{name}/build - Trigger builds without parameters
- POST /job/{name}/buildWithParameters - Trigger builds with parameters
- GET /job/{name}/{build_number}/api/json - Get build information
- GET /job/{name}/{build_number}/consoleText - Get build logs
- POST /job/{name}/{build_number}/stop - Cancel running builds
- POST /queue/item/{id}/cancelQueue - Cancel queued builds
- GET /queue/item/{id}/api/json - Get queue information
- GET /api/json - Health check endpoint

Error Handling:
- Comprehensive try/except blocks for ValueError and ConnectionError
- Authentication error detection (401/403 responses)
- Not found handling (404 responses)
- Retry logic with configurable attempts and delays
- Proper error logging at all levels

Features:
- Queue ID to build number tracking
- Support for folder paths in job names (/job/folder/job/job-name)
- Parameter passing (branch, commit_sha, custom parameters)
- Thread-safe run tracking with locks
- URL handling for Jenkins instances with custom base URLs
- SSL verification configuration

All verification tests passed successfully.

subtasks.description: Implement Jenkins job trigger via API/webhook

verification.instructions: Review code for trigger_job() method with proper API call to Jenkins

subtasks.notes:
Successfully verified trigger_job() method implementation:

Method Features:
- Accepts job_name, parameters, branch, and commit_sha parameters
- Makes POST requests to Jenkins API endpoints:
  - /job/{job_path}/buildWithParameters for parameterized builds
  - /job/{job_path}/build for simple builds
- Uses authenticated session (Basic auth or Bearer token)
- Extracts queue ID from response headers
- Creates and returns PipelineRun object with complete metadata
- Registers run using _register_run() for tracking
- Handles folder paths in job names (/job/folder/job/job-name)
- Adds BRANCH and COMMIT_SHA parameters automatically if not provided

Error Handling:
- Comprehensive try/except blocks for ValueError and ConnectionError
- Validates job_name presence before triggering
- Proper error logging at info and error levels
- Clear error messages for authentication failures

Code Quality:
- Detailed docstring with parameter descriptions, return type, raises, and usage examples
- Thread-safe implementation using locks
- No debug statements (verified: no print() or console.log)
- Follows existing code patterns and conventions

All verification checks passed:
✓ Method exists and is callable
✓ Has proper exception handling (ValueError, ConnectionError)
✓ Docstring describes API integration
✓ Makes proper POST requests to Jenkins API endpoints
✓ Returns PipelineRun object with all required fields
✓ Supports both parameterized and non-parameterized builds
✓ Extracts and tracks queue ID from response
✓ Registers run for tracking
✓ No debug print statements

subtasks.description: Implement Jenkins build status monitoring

verification.instructions: Review code for get_build_status() method with proper status polling

subtasks.notes:
Successfully implemented Jenkins build status monitoring with comprehensive polling capability:

Enhanced get_build_status() Method:
- Added poll parameter (default False) to enable continuous monitoring until build completion
- Added timeout parameter (default 600s) for maximum polling duration
- Added interval parameter (default 10s) for time between polls
- Implements polling loop that continues until build reaches terminal state (SUCCESS/FAILED/CANCELLED)
- Status change logging during polling for visibility into build progress
- Thread-safe updates to active_runs registry using locks
- Network error retry during polling with timeout handling
- Raises TimeoutError if build doesn't complete within timeout period
- Comprehensive docstring with parameter descriptions, return type, raises, and usage examples
- Follows GitHub Actions get_workflow_status() pattern for consistency across integrations

Implementation Details:
- Uses while True loop with conditional return based on poll parameter
- Checks for terminal states (SUCCESS, FAILED, CANCELLED) to exit polling
- Logs status changes during polling (e.g., "Build my-job #42 status: RUNNING (Jenkins: running)")
- Updates active_runs registry with current status and timestamp
- Handles network errors gracefully by retrying during polling
- Calculates elapsed time and raises TimeoutError if timeout exceeded
- Returns comprehensive status dictionary with all Jenkins build information

Error Handling:
- Comprehensive try/except blocks for ValueError, ConnectionError, and TimeoutError
- Network errors during polling trigger retry with logging
- Timeout handling with clear error messages including last status
- Proper error logging at all levels

Code Quality:
- No debug statements (verified: no print() or console.log)
- Follows existing code patterns and conventions
- Type hints for all parameters and return values
- Detailed documentation with examples

All verification checks passed:
✓ Method signature includes poll, timeout, and interval parameters
✓ Polling loop correctly implemented with while True
✓ Status change logging works correctly
✓ Network error handling with retries during polling
✓ Timeout handling raises TimeoutError
✓ Thread-safe updates to active_runs registry
✓ Returns detailed status information dictionary
✓ Comprehensive docstring with examples
✓ Import test successful
✓ No debug print statements

phases.name: Pipeline Monitoring Service
phases.description: Create a monitoring service that tracks all pipeline statuses and triggers actions

subtasks.description: Create PipelineMonitor class for tracking multiple pipelines

verification.command: python -c "from devflow.monitoring.pipeline_monitor import PipelineMonitor; print('PipelineMonitor OK')"

subtasks.notes:
Successfully implemented PipelineMonitor class with comprehensive monitoring capabilities:

Core Classes:
- StageStatus enum: PENDING, RUNNING, COMPLETED, FAILED, SKIPPED
- PipelineStage dataclass: Represents a stage within a CI/CD pipeline
- PipelineMonitor class: Main monitoring service

PipelineMonitor Features:
- __init__(): Initialize with StateTracker for system state integration
- start()/stop(): Control monitoring thread lifecycle
- track_pipeline(): Start tracking new pipelines with optional stage definitions
- start_pipeline()/complete_pipeline()/fail_pipeline(): Pipeline lifecycle management
- start_stage()/complete_stage()/fail_stage()/skip_stage(): Stage-level monitoring
- _monitor_loop(): Background monitoring for pipeline health checks
- _check_pipeline_health(): Detect stalled/timed-out pipelines
- get_pipeline_status()/get_all_pipelines()/get_running_pipelines(): Query methods
- get_pipeline_metrics(): Collect statistics (total, running, completed, failed, success_rate, avg_duration)
- Event callbacks: on_pipeline_start, on_pipeline_complete, on_pipeline_fail, on_stage_start, on_stage_complete

Thread Safety:
- All state updates use threading.Lock() for thread-safe operations
- Background monitoring loop runs as daemon thread
- Callback errors don't break monitoring (wrapped in try/except)

State Integration:
- Uses StateTracker for persistent pipeline state storage
- Creates and updates pipelines via StateTracker methods
- Reads pipeline state for monitoring and metrics
- Stage state stored in pipeline's stages list

Error Handling:
- Comprehensive exception handling throughout
- Timeout detection (1 hour default for running pipelines)
- Graceful handling of missing pipelines

Code Quality:
- Follows patterns from task_scheduler.py exactly
- Dataclasses for data structures
- Type hints on all methods
- Comprehensive docstrings with Args/Returns/Raises
- No debug statements (verified)

Verification:
✓ Import test passed: 'PipelineMonitor OK'
✓ All methods implemented with proper signatures
✓ Thread-safe implementation
✓ Event callback system working
✓ Pipeline health checks implemented
✓ Metrics collection functional
✓ Stage-level tracking complete
✓ Clean commit with descriptive message
✓ Implementation plan updated

subtasks.description: Implement status polling loop for active pipelines

verification.instructions: Review code for monitoring loop that polls pipeline statuses

subtasks.notes:
Successfully implemented active status polling for CI/CD pipelines:

Enhanced PipelineMonitor:
- __init__(): Added integrations parameter to accept CI/CD platform integrations
- start(): Added poll_interval parameter (default 30s) for configurable polling
- register_integration(): Dynamically register CI/CD platform integrations
- unregister_integration(): Remove integrations at runtime

New Polling Methods:
- _monitor_loop(): Enhanced to actively poll integrations every 30 seconds
- _poll_active_pipelines(): Poll all active (non-terminal) pipelines for status updates
  - Gets all pipelines from StateTracker
  - Filters for active pipelines (PENDING, QUEUED, RUNNING)
  - For each pipeline with an integration:
    - Retrieves integration instance from platform metadata
    - Gets external run_id from pipeline metadata
    - Calls integration.get_pipeline_status(run_id) to poll status
    - Maps integration status to StateTracker PipelineStatus
    - Updates StateTracker with new status
    - Triggers appropriate callbacks (on_pipeline_complete, on_pipeline_fail)
- _get_pipeline_integration(): Retrieve integration instance for a pipeline
- _map_integration_status(): Normalize status across different platforms (handles strings and enums)
- _update_pipeline_status_from_poll(): Update state and trigger callbacks on status changes

Enhanced track_pipeline():
- Added platform parameter to specify CI/CD platform (e.g., 'github', 'gitlab')
- Added run_id parameter to store external pipeline run ID
- Stores platform and run_id in pipeline metadata for polling

Enhanced StateTracker:
- Added metadata field to pipeline structure in create_pipeline()
- Added metadata parameter to update_pipeline_status() for metadata updates
- Supports storing platform and run_id for polling

Polling Behavior:
- Polls every 30 seconds (configurable via start(poll_interval=...))
- Only polls active (non-terminal) pipelines
- Skips pipelines without integration or run_id
- Continues polling even if individual polls fail
- Health checks run less frequently (every 60 seconds)
- Thread-safe integration registry access

Callback System:
- Triggers on_pipeline_complete callback when status becomes COMPLETED
- Triggers on_pipeline_fail callback when status becomes FAILED
- Callback errors don't break monitoring (wrapped in try/except)

Code Quality:
- Follows orchestrator.py monitoring loop pattern exactly
- Comprehensive error handling at all levels
- No debug statements (verified: no print() or console.log)
- Type hints for all parameters and return values
- Detailed docstrings with Args/Returns/Raises
- Thread-safe implementation with locks

Verification:
✓ All required methods implemented with correct signatures
✓ Polling loop correctly iterates active pipelines
✓ Integration retrieval works correctly
✓ Status mapping handles both strings and enums
✓ State updates trigger appropriate callbacks
✓ Metadata support in StateTracker
✓ Integration registry management works
✓ No syntax errors
✓ Import and initialization tests passed
✓ Clean commit with descriptive message

subtasks.description: Implement failure handling and auto-investigation task creation

verification.instructions: Review code for handling failed pipelines and creating investigation tasks

subtasks.notes:
Successfully implemented failure handling and auto-investigation task creation:

Added Dependencies:
- Imported TaskScheduler and TaskPriority from core.task_scheduler

Enhanced Constructor:
- Added task_scheduler parameter (optional) to PipelineMonitor
- Added _auto_investigate flag (enabled by default)
- Stored scheduler instance for task creation

Enhanced fail_pipeline():
- Added auto_investigate parameter to override setting per-failure
- Checks if auto-investigation is enabled and scheduler exists
- Calls _create_investigation_task() to create investigation task
- Maintains existing callback functionality

New _create_investigation_task() Method:
- Validates scheduler exists before proceeding
- Gets pipeline details from StateTracker
- Collects comprehensive failure context:
  - pipeline_id, pipeline_type, commit_sha, branch
  - error message
  - failed_stages (name, error, logs for each failed stage)
- Creates investigation task via scheduler.create_task():
  - task_type: 'investigation'
  - agent_type: 'investigator'
  - priority: TaskPriority.HIGH.value
  - input_data: failure_context, pipeline_id, investigation_type
- Stores investigation_task_id in pipeline metadata
- Comprehensive error handling (try/except with pass)

New set_auto_investigate() Method:
- Public method to enable/disable auto-investigation
- Updates _auto_investigate flag

Pattern Compliance:
- Follows orchestrator.py task creation pattern exactly
- Uses TaskPriority.HIGH.value for priority
- Uses scheduler.create_task() with proper parameters
- Thread-safe implementation
- No debug statements (verified)
- Type hints on all methods
- Comprehensive docstrings

Code Quality:
- No console.log/print debugging statements (verified)
- Proper error handling throughout
- Clean, readable code following existing patterns
- Maintains backward compatibility (scheduler is optional)

Verification:
✓ Imports added correctly
✓ Constructor updated with optional scheduler
✓ Auto-investigation flag added
✓ fail_pipeline() enhanced with investigation logic
✓ _create_investigation_task() properly implemented
✓ set_auto_investigate() method added
✓ Follows orchestrator.py patterns exactly
✓ Error handling in place
✓ No debug statements
✓ Python syntax check passed
✓ Clean commit with descriptive message

phases.name: End-to-End Integration
phases.description: Wire all components together and verify end-to-end CI/CD flow

subtasks.description: Create end-to-end test workflow for GitHub Actions

steps: Create test GitHub repository with workflow file
Use DevFlow to make a code change
Verify GitHub Actions workflow is triggered
Monitor pipeline status
Verify status is tracked in StateTracker

subtasks.description: Create end-to-end test workflow for GitLab CI

steps: Create test GitLab repository with pipeline file
Use DevFlow to make a code change
Verify GitLab CI pipeline is triggered
Monitor pipeline status
Verify status is tracked in StateTracker

subtasks.description: Create end-to-end test workflow for Jenkins

steps: Create test Jenkins job
Use DevFlow to make a code change
Verify Jenkins job is triggered
Monitor build status
Verify status is tracked in StateTracker

subtasks.description: Verify failure handling and auto-investigation

steps: Create a failing CI/CD pipeline
Trigger pipeline with DevFlow
Verify failure is detected
Verify investigation task is created
Verify failure is logged

acceptance_criteria: All unit tests pass for integration classes
Integration tests pass with test repositories
End-to-end tests demonstrate full CI/CD flow
No secrets or credentials in code
GitHub Actions integration works out of the box
GitLab CI integration works out of the box
Jenkins integration works out of the box
verification_strategy.reasoning: High risk change involving external API integrations and credential management. Requires comprehensive testing at all levels - unit tests for individual classes, integration tests for API interactions, and end-to-end tests for the complete flow. Security scanning is critical because credentials and secrets are involved.

checks: HTTPS used for all API calls
Proper error handling for authentication failures

parallel_groups.reason: All three platform integrations depend only on phase-1-base and work in different files
